tossups.question: In a painting from this country, a habit-wearing priest on a seashore leans over a man who is naked other than his broken blue and red-feathered loin cloth; that painting from this country compositionally quotes an earlier painting from this country titled Moema. While on an expedition from Austria, Thomas Ender painted landscapes near this country’s largest city, where Jean-Baptiste Debret captured the notion of banzo in his portraits of Black people. Romantic art in this country is periodized into (*) Indianism, Ultra-Romantic, and Condorist sub-movements. Art from this country shown in the Lina Bo Bardi-designed MASP includes Concrete and Constructivist art by Lygia Pape and Lygia Clark. For 10 points, name this country where artists like Victor Meirelles and Pedro Américo painted paulistas landscapes of its largest city.
tossups.question_sanitized: In a painting from this country, a habit-wearing priest on a seashore leans over a man who is naked other than his broken blue and red-feathered loin cloth; that painting from this country compositionally quotes an earlier painting from this country titled Moema. While on an expedition from Austria, Thomas Ender painted landscapes near this country's largest city, where Jean-Baptiste Debret captured the notion of banzo in his portraits of Black people. Romantic art in this country is periodized into (*) Indianism, Ultra-Romantic, and Condorist sub-movements. Art from this country shown in the Lina Bo Bardi-designed MASP includes Concrete and Constructivist art by Lygia Pape and Lygia Clark. For 10 points, name this country where artists like Victor Meirelles and Pedro Americo painted paulistas landscapes of its largest city.
tossups.answer: Brazil [or Federative Republic of Brazil or República Federativa do Brasil] (The painting in the first line is The Last Tamoio by Rodolfo Amoedo.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Brazil [or Federative Republic of Brazil or Republica Federativa do Brasil] (The painting in the first line is The Last Tamoio by Rodolfo Amoedo.)

tossups.question: One artist from this country painted a scene in which a solitary person sits on an upturned road, as the title Windstorm rages in the distance. That same artist depicted a horse nudging the remains of a bloodstained man holding a gun in The End of Romance. A conceptual artist from this country created odd face coverings, which include her Abyss Masks, Dialogue Goggles, and Sensorial Masks. (*) Another artist from this country painted a person seated next to a cactus from an extremely distorted perspective, with enlarged hands and feet. That painting, Abaporu, was painted by a member of the Grupo dos Cinco, which included the author of the Anthropophagic Manifesto. For 10 points, name this home country of Antonio Parreiras, Lygia Clark, and Tarsila do Amaral.
tossups.question_sanitized: One artist from this country painted a scene in which a solitary person sits on an upturned road, as the title Windstorm rages in the distance. That same artist depicted a horse nudging the remains of a bloodstained man holding a gun in The End of Romance. A conceptual artist from this country created odd face coverings, which include her Abyss Masks, Dialogue Goggles, and Sensorial Masks. (*) Another artist from this country painted a person seated next to a cactus from an extremely distorted perspective, with enlarged hands and feet. That painting, Abaporu, was painted by a member of the Grupo dos Cinco, which included the author of the Anthropophagic Manifesto. For 10 points, name this home country of Antonio Parreiras, Lygia Clark, and Tarsila do Amaral.

tossups.question: An artist from this country created a piece that requires two participants to bind their hands together with a Möbius strip as part of the Nostalgia of the Body series. An artist from this country who died of lead poisoning painted the War and Peace panels at the UN Headquarters. Lygia Clark was a leading member of this country’s Neo-Concrete movement. An artist from this country depicted a woman with giant feet next to a cactus in the painting Abaporu. A hyperboloid cathedral made from concrete columns sits in this country’s bird- or airplane-shaped capital city. A 98 foot-tall concrete and soapstone sculpture atop a mountain in this country depicts a religious figure with his arms open. For 10 points, name this home country of Tarsila do Amaral, which contains the Christ the Redeemer statue.
tossups.answer: Brazil [or Brasil; or Federative Republic of Brazil or República Federativa do Brasil] (The second line refers to Candido Portinari.)

packet.name: H_ McGill, Johns Hopkins, UNC B, Oklahoma

tossups.answer_sanitized: Brazil [or Brasil; or Federative Republic of Brazil or Republica Federativa do Brasil] (The second line refers to Candido Portinari.)

tossups.question: A manifesto from this country argues that art is a “quasi-corpus.” One artist from this country created hinged metal works that visitors are invited to manipulate. That artist of Bichos (“BEE-shooz”), Lygia Clark, helped found this country’s Neo-Concrete movement. Paul Landowski designed an Art Deco sculpture in this country made of concrete covered in soapstone tiles. An interactive installation originated the name of this country’s (*) Tropicália movement. A reflecting pool cools a building resembling a crown from this country, which is ringed by sixteen hyperboloid columns. That cathedral was designed along with other buildings in this country’s capital city by Oscar Niemeyer. For 10 points, name this country where Heitor da Silva Costa built the Christ the Redeemer statue.
tossups.answer: Brazil [or Federative Republic of Brazil or República Federativa do Brasil] (The first line refers to the Neo-Concrete Manifesto. Tropicália was created by the Neo-Concrete artist Hélio Oiticica.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Brazil [or Federative Republic of Brazil or Republica Federativa do Brasil] (The first line refers to the Neo-Concrete Manifesto. Tropicalia was created by the Neo-Concrete artist Helio Oiticica.)
tossups.question_sanitized: A manifesto from this country argues that art is a "quasi-corpus." One artist from this country created hinged metal works that visitors are invited to manipulate. That artist of Bichos ("BEE-shooz"), Lygia Clark, helped found this country's Neo-Concrete movement. Paul Landowski designed an Art Deco sculpture in this country made of concrete covered in soapstone tiles. An interactive installation originated the name of this country's (*) Tropicalia movement. A reflecting pool cools a building resembling a crown from this country, which is ringed by sixteen hyperboloid columns. That cathedral was designed along with other buildings in this country's capital city by Oscar Niemeyer. For 10 points, name this country where Heitor da Silva Costa built the Christ the Redeemer statue.

tossups.question: An artist from this country created a huge cotton sheet with evenly-spaced holes for people to put their heads in, entitled Divisor. A retrospective of Swiss artist Max Bill in this country is credited with starting its modern sculpture movement. An artist from this country created explorable sets of panels called Penetrables and colorful capes called Parangolés. Instructions for cutting up a paper Möbius strip make up the work Walking by another artist from this country. That artist from this country used a word meaning “Critter” to name sculptures consisting of aluminum plates that viewers could move on their hinges. The Neo-Concrete movement in this country was led by the sculptors (*) Lygia Pape, Lygia Clark, and Hélio Oiticica. French artist Paul Landowski sculpted a concrete and soapstone Art Deco statue located on a mountain peak in this country. For 10 points, name this country where Heitor da Silva Costa designed the statue Christ the Redeemer.
tossups.question_sanitized: An artist from this country created a huge cotton sheet with evenly-spaced holes for people to put their heads in, entitled Divisor. A retrospective of Swiss artist Max Bill in this country is credited with starting its modern sculpture movement. An artist from this country created explorable sets of panels called Penetrables and colorful capes called Parangoles. Instructions for cutting up a paper Mobius strip make up the work Walking by another artist from this country. That artist from this country used a word meaning "Critter" to name sculptures consisting of aluminum plates that viewers could move on their hinges. The Neo-Concrete movement in this country was led by the sculptors (*) Lygia Pape, Lygia Clark, and Helio Oiticica. French artist Paul Landowski sculpted a concrete and soapstone Art Deco statue located on a mountain peak in this country. For 10 points, name this country where Heitor da Silva Costa designed the statue Christ the Redeemer.

bonuses.leadin: Hélio Oiticica (“AY-lee-oo oy-chee-SEE-kuh”), Lygia Clark (“LEE-zhuh clark”), and several other Brazilian artists of the Neoconcrete movement were inspired by a 1951 retrospective on a student of this school named Max Bill. For 10 points each:

parts: Paul Klee (“clay”) and Wassily Kandinsky taught at what functionalist, Weimar-era design school founded in Germany by Walter Gropius?

Oiticica’s Cosmacocas installations were inspired by his cocaine highs, during which he wrote an ode dedicated to this Romanian sculptor of The Endless Column and Bird in Space.

bonuses.leadin: English or foreign-language term acceptable. An indigenous term for a performer of this action titles a 1928 painting in which a man with a cartoonishly large foot sits next to a cactus beneath a yellow sun. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this practice performed by the title figure of that Tarsila do Amaral (“ah-mah-ROW”) painting. Tarsila’s husband Oswald de Andrade (“jee ahn-DRAH-jee”) was inspired by that painting to write an artistic manifesto titled for this practice.
Tarsila do Amaral was a modern artist from this country, whose more conventional 20th-century art includes the massive sculpture Christ the Redeemer.

The Brazilian artist Lygia Clark had participants slowly remove this material from their mouths in the work Anthropophagic Drool. Objects are covered with this material in a type of street art called its namesake “bombing.”
answers: cannibalism [or anthropophagy or antropófago; accept Manifesto Antropófago; accept Abaporu; or equivalents like eating humans; prompt on eating]
Brazil [or Brasil or Federative Republic of Brazil or República Federativa do Brasil]
yarn [or string or thread or twine or equivalents; accept wool; accept yarn bombing]

bonuses.leadin: A 2017 Met Breuer exhibition of a Brazilian artist with this given name titled “A Multitude of Forms” staged a piece in which a crowd walk together with their heads protruding from a huge white sheet. For 10 points each:
parts: Give this first name shared by the artist of Divisor and a different Brazilian artist who gave a title meaning “critters” to a set of hinged metal assemblies meant to be manipulated by viewers.

Lygias Pape and Clark led an art movement that prefixes this word with “neo.” Lygia Fagundes Telles and the de Campos brothers belonged to a literary school named for this word, which describes poems whose typographical form conveys meaning.

Lygia Clark’s work Walking consists of a paper with this shape that viewers cut lengthwise repeatedly until it is too thin to cut. This non-orientable shape is constructed by giving the paper a half-twist and taping its ends.
answers: Lygia (“LEE-zhuh”) [accept Lygia Pape or Lygia Clark] (The Lygia Clark work is Bichos.)

bonuses.leadin: This artist portrayed a green demonic dog bloodying the back of a blindfolded prisoner in a prison cell as part of his series condemning the Abu Ghraib scandal, which he called "a permanent accusation." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Colombian artist who, in his own versions of the Mona Lisa and Arnolfini Wedding, represented the subjects in his characteristic style, with rotund bodies and exaggerated volumes.

This Mexican painter included serpents rising out of the sea around a pointing figure in Departure of Quetzalcoatl, part of a series he did for Dartmouth College entitled The Epic of American Civilization.

Artists from this Latin American nation include Lygia Clark, who made a series of movable metal objects with hinges called Bichos. This nation’s Mt. Corcovado is topped by the massive Art Deco statue Christ the Redeemer.
answers: Federative Republic of Brazil [or República Federativa do Brasil]
